name: revise
summary: This command is used to set or change properties of an already existing object.
syntax: The syntax of this command for object created with "single line" command is revise id [-option1 value1 -option2 value2 ...] For objects created with "multi line" commands is syntax following revise id [-option1 value1 -option2 value2 ...] ... optionX valueX data ... endrevise

arguments.name: <id>
arguments.description: identifier of the object to revise

options.raw: adjust <horizontal/vertical> = shifts the line point to be aligned horizontally/ver\-ti\-cally with the previous point. It can't be set on the first point. The result is a horizontal/vertical line segment. This option is not allowed in the plan projection.
options.name: adjust <horizontal/vertical>
options.description: shifts the line point to be aligned horizontally/ver\-ti\-cally with the previous point. It can't be set on the first point. The result is a horizontal/vertical line segment. This option is not allowed in the plan projection.

options.raw: align = alignment of the symbol or text. The following values are accepted: center, c, top, t, bottom, b, left, l, right, r, top-left, tl, top-right, tr, bottom-left, bl, bottom-right, br.
options.name: align
options.description: alignment of the symbol or text. The following values are accepted: center, c, top, t, bottom, b, left, l, right, r, top-left, tl, top-right, tr, bottom-left, bl, bottom-right, br.

options.raw: altitude <value> = can be specified only with the wall type. This option creates an altitude label on the wall. All altitudes are exported as a difference against grid Z origin (which is 0 by default). If the value is specified, it gives the altitude difference of the point on the wall relative to the nearest station. The value will be set to 0 if defined as "-", ".", "nan", "NAN" or "NaN". The value can be prefixed by a keyword "fix", then no nearest station is taken into consideration; the absolute given value is used instead. Units can follow the value. Examples: +4, [+4 m], [fix 1510 m].
options.name: altitude <value>
options.description: can be specified only with the wall type. This option creates an altitude label on the wall. All altitudes are exported as a difference against grid Z origin (which is 0 by default). If the value is specified, it gives the altitude difference of the point on the wall relative to the nearest station. The value will be set to 0 if defined as "-", ".", "nan", "NAN" or "NaN". The value can be prefixed by a keyword "fix", then no nearest station is taken into consideration; the absolute given value is used instead. Units can follow the value. Examples: +4, [+4 m], [fix 1510 m].

options.raw: anchors <on/off> = this option can be specified only with the "rope" line type. Default is on.
options.name: anchors <on/off>
options.description: this option can be specified only with the "rope" line type. Default is on.

options.raw: attributes <(on)/off> = set whether to export user defined attributes in continuation list table.
options.name: attributes <(on)/off>
options.description: set whether to export user defined attributes in continuation list table.

options.raw: author <date> <person> = author of the data and its creation date
options.name: author <date> <person>
options.description: author of the data and its creation date

options.raw: base-scale <picture length> <real length> = if set, Therion will optically scale the map by a (scale/base-scale) factor. This has the same effect as if the map printed in base-scale would be photo-reduced to the scale. (M, A)
options.name: base-scale <picture length> <real length>
options.description: if set, Therion will optically scale the map by a (scale/base-scale) factor. This has the same effect as if the map printed in base-scale would be photo-reduced to the scale. (M, A)

options.raw: bitmap <filename> <calibration> = scanned topographical map. calibration may have two forms: 1. [X1 Y1 x1 y1 X2 Y2 x2 y2 [units]], where upper case X/Y variables are picture coordinates (pixels; lower-left corner is 0 0), lower-case x/y variables are real coordinates. Optional units apply to real coordinates (metres by default). 2. [X1 Y1 station1 X2 Y2 station2], where upper case X/Y variables are picture coordinates and station1 and station2 are survey stations names.
options.name: bitmap <filename> <calibration>
options.description: scanned topographical map. calibration may have two forms: 1. [X1 Y1 x1 y1 X2 Y2 x2 y2 [units]], where upper case X/Y variables are picture coordinates (pixels; lower-left corner is 0 0), lower-case x/y variables are real coordinates. Optional units apply to real coordinates (metres by default). 2. [X1 Y1 station1 X2 Y2 station2], where upper case X/Y variables are picture coordinates and station1 and station2 are survey stations names.

options.raw: border <on/off> = this option can be specified only with the "slope" symbol type. It switches on/off the border line of the slope.
options.name: border <on/off>
options.description: this option can be specified only with the "slope" symbol type. It switches on/off the border line of the slope.

options.raw: calibrate [<x> <y> <z> <X> <Y> <Z>] = coordinates in the imported file are shifted from lower-case coordinates to upper-case coordinates.
options.name: calibrate [<x> <y> <z> <X> <Y> <Z>]
options.description: coordinates in the imported file are shifted from lower-case coordinates to upper-case coordinates.

options.raw: chapter-level <number> = valid only when a map is selected. Determines the level at which chapter expansion for atlas export is stopped. By default 0 is used, if "-" or "." is specified, no chapter is exported for this map. If title-pages option in layout is on, each chapter starts with a title page.
options.name: chapter-level <number>
options.description: valid only when a map is selected. Determines the level at which chapter expansion for atlas export is stopped. By default 0 is used, if "-" or "." is specified, no chapter is exported for this map. If title-pages option in layout is on, each chapter starts with a title page.

options.raw: clip <on/off> = specify whether a symbol is clipped by the scrap border.
options.name: clip <on/off>
options.description: specify whether a symbol is clipped by the scrap border.

options.raw: close <on/off/auto> = determines whether a line is closed or not
options.name: close <on/off/auto>
options.description: determines whether a line is closed or not

options.raw: code <metapost/tex-map/tex-atlas> = Add/redefine \ and \ macros here. This allows user to configure various things (like user defined symbols, map and atlas layout at one place &c.) See the chapter Changing layout for details.
options.name: code <metapost/tex-map/tex-atlas>
options.description: Add/redefine \ and \ macros here. This allows user to configure various things (like user defined symbols, map and atlas layout at one place &c.) See the chapter Changing layout for details.

options.raw: colo[u]r <color> = customize colour for special map items (map-fg, map-bg, preview-above, preview-below, labels). Colour range is 0--100 for grayscale, [0--100 0--100 0--100] triplet for RGB colours and [0--100 0--100 0--100 0--100] quadruplet for CMYK colours. (Note, that not all colour combinations are valid; e.g. no printer will print CMYK [100 100 100 100 93. The maximum ink coverage or limit ( C+M+Y+K ) may be around 240 or 300, depending on the printer.) RGB colours can be specified in hexadecimal format (e.g. ffeeaa). For map-fg, you can use altitude, scrap or map as colours. In this case the map is coloured according to altitude, scraps or maps. For map-bg, you can use transparent to omit page background completely. For labels, you can switch colour on/off. If on, labels are coloured using the colour of associated scrap.
options.name: colo[u]r <color>
options.description: customize colour for special map items (map-fg, map-bg, preview-above, preview-below, labels). Colour range is 0--100 for grayscale, [0--100 0--100 0--100] triplet for RGB colours and [0--100 0--100 0--100 0--100] quadruplet for CMYK colours. (Note, that not all colour combinations are valid; e.g. no printer will print CMYK [100 100 100 100 93. The maximum ink coverage or limit ( C+M+Y+K ) may be around 240 or 300, depending on the printer.) RGB colours can be specified in hexadecimal format (e.g. ffeeaa). For map-fg, you can use altitude, scrap or map as colours. In this case the map is coloured according to altitude, scraps or maps. For map-bg, you can use transparent to omit page background completely. For labels, you can switch colour on/off. If on, labels are coloured using the colour of associated scrap.

options.raw: colo[u]r-legend <smooth/discrete/off/on> = set type of map-fg colours legend when map-fg is set to altitude, scrap or map. For compatibility reasons, on is equivalent to smooth. (M, A; default: smooth if applicable)
options.name: colo[u]r-legend <smooth/discrete/off/on>
options.description: set type of map-fg colours legend when map-fg is set to altitude, scrap or map. For compatibility reasons, on is equivalent to smooth. (M, A; default: smooth if applicable)

options.raw: colo[u]r-model <cmyk/rgb/grayscale> = select the output colour model (M, A; default: cmyk). The CMYK colour model is intended for printing. Black overprint is used for the best appearance of black lines and texts. The hue of other colours may vary depending on the type or settings of the RIP or printer driver. The RGB colour model is intended for screen or multimedia projector presentation. If used for printing, the result will depend on the colour management settings and also on particular colour, as not all RGB colours can be represented on printer. The grayscale colour model is meant for printing on B&W printers. If input colours (lookup, colour in the layout) do not match the output colour model (e.g. only RGB is defined in the lookup and CMYK selected as the colour-model), they will be converted to the output model.
options.name: colo[u]r-model <cmyk/rgb/grayscale>
options.description: select the output colour model (M, A; default: cmyk). The CMYK colour model is intended for printing. Black overprint is used for the best appearance of black lines and texts. The hue of other colours may vary depending on the type or settings of the RIP or printer driver. The RGB colour model is intended for screen or multimedia projector presentation. If used for printing, the result will depend on the colour management settings and also on particular colour, as not all RGB colours can be represented on printer. The grayscale colour model is meant for printing on B&W printers. If input colours (lookup, colour in the layout) do not match the output colour model (e.g. only RGB is defined in the lookup and CMYK selected as the colour-model), they will be converted to the output model.

options.raw: colo[u]r-profile <cmyk/rgb/grayscale> <filename> = assign an ICC profile to colours specified in the given colour model. ICC profiles are applied only to drawings, not to embedded raster images like sketches and surface bitmaps. (M, A)
options.name: colo[u]r-profile <cmyk/rgb/grayscale> <filename>
options.description: assign an ICC profile to colours specified in the given colour model. ICC profiles are applied only to drawings, not to embedded raster images like sketches and surface bitmaps. (M, A)

options.raw: context <point/line/area> <symbol-type> = (to be used with symbol-hide and symbol-show layout options) symbol will be hidden/shown according to rules for specified <symbol-type>.
options.name: context <point/line/area> <symbol-type>
options.description: (to be used with symbol-hide and symbol-show layout options) symbol will be hidden/shown according to rules for specified <symbol-type>.

options.raw: copy <source layout id> = set properties here that are not modified based on the given source layout. map presentation-related:
options.name: copy <source layout id>
options.description: set properties here that are not modified based on the given source layout. map presentation-related:

options.raw: copyright <date> <string> = copyright date and name
options.name: copyright <date> <string>
options.description: copyright date and name

options.raw: count <N> (when used with scraps) = Therion will try to join scraps which connect in N locations/passages.
options.name: count <N> (when used with scraps)
options.description: Therion will try to join scraps which connect in N locations/passages.

options.raw: cs <coordinate system> = assumes that (calibrated) local scrap coordinates are given in specified coordinate system. It is useful for absolute placing of imported sketches where no survey stations are specified. (If there are some survey stations in the scrap, the cs specification is ignored.)
options.name: cs <coordinate system>
options.description: assumes that (calibrated) local scrap coordinates are given in specified coordinate system. It is useful for absolute placing of imported sketches where no survey stations are specified. (If there are some survey stations in the scrap, the cs specification is ignored.)

options.raw: -d = Turn on debugging mode. The current implementation creates a temporary directory named thTMPDIR (in your system temporary directory) and does not delete any temporary files.
options.name: -d
options.description: Turn on debugging mode. The current implementation creates a temporary directory named thTMPDIR (in your system temporary directory) and does not delete any temporary files.

options.raw: debug <on/all/first/second/scrap-names/station-names/off> = draw scrap in different stages of transformation in different colours to see how Therion distorts map data. See the description of scrap command for details. The points with distance changed most during transformation are displayed orange. If scrap-names is specified, scrap names are shown for each scrap, station-names displays name of each survey station.
options.name: debug <on/all/first/second/scrap-names/station-names/off>
options.description: draw scrap in different stages of transformation in different colours to see how Therion distorts map data. See the description of scrap command for details. The points with distance changed most during transformation are displayed orange. If scrap-names is specified, scrap names are shown for each scrap, station-names displays name of each survey station.

options.raw: declination <specification> = set the default declination for all data objects in this survey (which can be overridden by declination definitions in subsurveys). The <specification> has three forms: 1. [] an empty string. This will reset the declination definition. 2. [<value> <units>] will set a single value (also for undated surveys). 3. [<date1> <value1> [<date2> <value2> ... ] <units>] will set declination for several dates. Then the declination of each shot will be set according to the date specification of the data object. If you want to explicitly set the declination for undated survey data, use "-" instead of date. If no declination is specified and some geodetic coordinate system is defined, the declination is automatically computed using built-in geomagnetic model. N.B.: The declination is positive when the magnetic north is east of true north.
options.name: declination <specification>
options.description: set the default declination for all data objects in this survey (which can be overridden by declination definitions in subsurveys). The <specification> has three forms: 1. [] an empty string. This will reset the declination definition. 2. [<value> <units>] will set a single value (also for undated surveys). 3. [<date1> <value1> [<date2> <value2> ... ] <units>] will set declination for several dates. Then the declination of each shot will be set according to the date specification of the data object. If you want to explicitly set the declination for undated survey data, use "-" instead of date. If no declination is specified and some geodetic coordinate system is defined, the declination is automatically computed using built-in geomagnetic model. N.B.: The declination is positive when the magnetic north is east of true north.

options.raw: direction <begin/end/both/none/point> = can be used only with the section type. It indicates where to put a direction arrow on the section line. None is default. The point option must be used inside [LINE DATA]. The others can (and should) be used as a line option.
options.name: direction <begin/end/both/none/point>
options.description: can be used only with the section type. It indicates where to put a direction arrow on the section line. None is default. The point option must be used inside [LINE DATA]. The others can (and should) be used as a line option.

options.raw: disable <walls/[cave/surface-]centerline/splay-shots/surface/all> = selects which features to export, if the format supports it. Surface is currently exported in therion format only.
options.name: disable <walls/[cave/surface-]centerline/splay-shots/surface/all>
options.description: selects which features to export, if the format supports it. Surface is currently exported in therion format only.

options.raw: dist <distance> = if the point type is extra, specifies the distance to the nearest station (or station specified using -from option. If not specified, appropriate value from LRUD data is used.
options.name: dist <distance>
options.description: if the point type is extra, specifies the distance to the nearest station (or station specified using -from option. If not specified, appropriate value from LRUD data is used.

options.raw: doc-author <string> = set document author (M, A)
options.name: doc-author <string>
options.description: set document author (M, A)

options.raw: doc-keywords <string> = set document keywords (M, A)
options.name: doc-keywords <string>
options.description: set document keywords (M, A)

options.raw: doc-subject <string> = set document subject (M, A)
options.name: doc-subject <string>
options.description: set document subject (M, A)

options.raw: doc-title <string> = set document title (M, A) customization:
options.name: doc-title <string>
options.description: set document title (M, A) customization:

options.raw: enable <walls/[cave/surface-]centerline/splay-shots/surface/all> = object-specific option accepted by revise; semantics depend on the revised object type.
options.name: enable <walls/[cave/surface-]centerline/splay-shots/surface/all>
options.description: object-specific option accepted by revise; semantics depend on the revised object type.

options.raw: encoding/enc <encoding> = set output encoding
options.name: encoding/enc <encoding>
options.description: set output encoding

options.raw: endcode = should end the TeX\ and \ sections
options.name: endcode
options.description: should end the TeX\ and \ sections

options.raw: entrance <station-name> = specifies the main entrance to the cave represented by this survey. If not specified and there is exactly one station marked entrance in this survey, it is considered to represent a cave also. This information is used for cave-list export.
options.name: entrance <station-name>
options.description: specifies the main entrance to the cave represented by this survey. If not specified and there is exactly one station marked entrance in this survey, it is considered to represent a cave also. This information is used for cave-list export.

options.raw: exclude-pages <on/off> <list> = exclude specified pages from cave atlas. The list may contain page numbers separated by a comma or dash (for intervals) e.g. 2,4-7,9,23 means, that pages 2, 4, 5, 6, 7, 9 and 23 should be omitted. Only the map pages should be counted. (Set own-pages 0 and title-pages off to get the correct page numbers to be excluded.) Changes of own-pages or title-pages options don't affect page excluding. (A)
options.name: exclude-pages <on/off> <list>
options.description: exclude specified pages from cave atlas. The list may contain page numbers separated by a comma or dash (for intervals) e.g. 2,4-7,9,23 means, that pages 2, 4, 5, 6, 7, 9 and 23 should be omitted. Only the map pages should be counted. (Set own-pages 0 and title-pages off to get the correct page numbers to be excluded.) Changes of own-pages or title-pages options don't affect page excluding. (A)

options.raw: explored <length> = if the point type is continuation, you can specify length of passages explored but not surveyed yet. This value is afterwards displayed in survey/cave statistics.
options.name: explored <length>
options.description: if the point type is continuation, you can specify length of passages explored but not surveyed yet. This value is afterwards displayed in survey/cave statistics.

options.raw: extend [prev[ious] <station>] = if the point type is station and scrap projection is extended elevation, you can adjust the extension of the centreline using this option.
options.name: extend [prev[ious] <station>]
options.description: if the point type is station and scrap projection is extended elevation, you can adjust the extension of the centreline using this option.

options.raw: filter <(on)/off> = if specified, only stations with given prefix and shots between them will be imported. Prefix will be removed from station names.
options.name: filter <(on)/off>
options.description: if specified, only stations with given prefix and shots between them will be imported. Prefix will be removed from station names.

options.raw: flip (none)/horizontal/vertical = flips the scrap after scale transformation
options.name: flip (none)/horizontal/vertical
options.description: flips the scrap after scale transformation

options.raw: fonts-setup <tinysize> <smallsize> <normalsize> <largesize> <hugesize> = specify size of the text in points. <normalsize> applies to point label, <smallsize> applies to remark and all other point labels. Each of them may apply to line label according to its -scale option. The defaults are 8 10 12 16 24 for scales upto 1:100; 7 8 10 14 20 for scales upto 1:200; 6 7 8 10 14 for scales upto 1:500 and 5 6 7 8 10 for scales smaller than 1:500.
options.name: fonts-setup <tinysize> <smallsize> <normalsize> <largesize> <hugesize>
options.description: specify size of the text in points. <normalsize> applies to point label, <smallsize> applies to remark and all other point labels. Each of them may apply to line label according to its -scale option. The defaults are 8 10 12 16 24 for scales upto 1:100; 7 8 10 14 20 for scales upto 1:200; 6 7 8 10 14 for scales upto 1:500 and 5 6 7 8 10 for scales smaller than 1:500.

options.raw: format/fmt <format> = set model output format. Currently the following output formats are supported: loch (native format; default), compass (plt file), survex (3d file), dxf, esri (3d shapefiles), vrml, 3dmf and kml (Google Earth).
options.name: format/fmt <format>
options.description: set model output format. Currently the following output formats are supported: loch (native format; default), compass (plt file), survex (3d file), dxf, esri (3d shapefiles), vrml, 3dmf and kml (Google Earth).

options.raw: from <station> = if the point type is extra, specifies reference station.
options.name: from <station>
options.description: if the point type is extra, specifies reference station.

options.raw: --generate-output-crc = Generate a ".crc" file with CRC32 checksum for each output file. Implies --reproducible-output.
options.name: --generate-output-crc
options.description: Generate a ".crc" file with CRC32 checksum for each output file. Implies --reproducible-output.

options.raw: geospatial <on/off> = enable/disable geospatial PDF features. When enabled, compatible applications can use this information (e.g.\ Acrobat Reader for length measurements and finding coordinates; GIS applications like GDAL (Raster GDAL tools like gdal_translate work fine, while vector tools like ogr2ogr support Therion maps starting from version 3.10.0. Note that QGIS uses GDAL library to import PDF data.) and QGIS for importing and processing georeferenced data). This feature should be used with layers on setting. (M; default: on)
options.name: geospatial <on/off>
options.description: enable/disable geospatial PDF features. When enabled, compatible applications can use this information (e.g.\ Acrobat Reader for length measurements and finding coordinates; GIS applications like GDAL (Raster GDAL tools like gdal_translate work fine, while vector tools like ogr2ogr support Therion maps starting from version 3.10.0. Note that QGIS uses GDAL library to import PDF data.) and QGIS for importing and processing georeferenced data). This feature should be used with layers on setting. (M; default: on)

options.raw: gradient <none/center/point> = can be used only with the contour type and indicates where to put a gradient mark on the contour line. If there is no gradient specification, behaviour is symbol-set dependent (e.g. no tick in UIS, tick in the middle in SKBB). The point option must be used inside [LINE DATA]. The others can (and should) be used as a line option.
options.name: gradient <none/center/point>
options.description: can be used only with the contour type and indicates where to put a gradient mark on the contour line. If there is no gradient specification, behaviour is symbol-set dependent (e.g. no tick in UIS, tick in the middle in SKBB). The point option must be used inside [LINE DATA]. The others can (and should) be used as a line option.

options.raw: grid <off/bottom/top> = enable/disable grid (optionally coordinates' values may be also displayed) (M, A; default: off)
options.name: grid <off/bottom/top>
options.description: enable/disable grid (optionally coordinates' values may be also displayed) (M, A; default: off)

options.raw: grid-coords <off/border/all> = specify where to label grid with coordinates. (M, A; default: off)
options.name: grid-coords <off/border/all>
options.description: specify where to label grid with coordinates. (M, A; default: off)

options.raw: grid-flip (none)/vertical/horizontal = useful if your grid (exported from other program) needs to be flipped
options.name: grid-flip (none)/vertical/horizontal
options.description: useful if your grid (exported from other program) needs to be flipped

options.raw: grid-origin <x> <y> <z> <units> = set coordinates of grid origin (M, A)
options.name: grid-origin <x> <y> <z> <units>
options.description: set coordinates of grid origin (M, A)

options.raw: grid-size <x> <y> <z> <units> = set grid size in real units (M, A; default is equal to scalebar size)
options.name: grid-size <x> <y> <z> <units>
options.description: set grid size in real units (M, A; default is equal to scalebar size)

options.raw: grid-units <units> = units in which grid is specified. Metres by default.
options.name: grid-units <units>
options.description: units in which grid is specified. Metres by default.

options.raw: -h, --help = Display short help.
options.name: -h, --help
options.description: Display short help.

options.raw: head <begin/end/both/none> = can be used only with the arrow type and indicates where to put an arrow head. End is default.
options.name: head <begin/end/both/none>
options.description: can be used only with the arrow type and indicates where to put an arrow head. End is default.

options.raw: height <value> = height of pit or wall:pit; available in \ as a numeric variable ATTR__height.
options.name: height <value>
options.description: height of pit or wall:pit; available in \ as a numeric variable ATTR__height.

options.raw: id <ext_keyword> = ID of the symbol.
options.name: id <ext_keyword>
options.description: ID of the symbol.

options.raw: -L = Change the name of the log file.
options.name: -L
options.description: Change the name of the log file.

options.raw: l-size <number> = Size of the line (to the left). Only valid on and required for slope type.
options.name: l-size <number>
options.description: Size of the line (to the left). Only valid on and required for slope type.

options.raw: language <xx[_YY]> = set output language. Available languages are listed on the copyright page. See the Appendix if you want to add or customize translations. (M, A)
options.name: language <xx[_YY]>
options.description: set output language. Available languages are listed on the copyright page. See the Appendix if you want to add or customize translations. (M, A)

options.raw: layers <on/off> = enable/disable PDF 1.5 layers (M, A; default: on)
options.name: layers <on/off>
options.description: enable/disable PDF 1.5 layers (M, A; default: on)

options.raw: layout <id> = use predefined map or atlas layout.
options.name: layout <id>
options.description: use predefined map or atlas layout.

options.raw: layout-xxx = where xxx stands for other layout options. Using this you can change some layout properties directly within the export command.
options.name: layout-xxx
options.description: where xxx stands for other layout options. Using this you can change some layout properties directly within the export command.

options.raw: legend <on/off/all> = display list of used map symbols in the map header. If set to all, all symbols from the current symbol set are displayed. (M, A; default: off)
options.name: legend <on/off/all>
options.description: display list of used map symbols in the map header. If set to all, all symbols from the current symbol set are displayed. (M, A; default: off)

options.raw: legend-columns <number> = adjusts the number of legend columns (M, A; default: 2)
options.name: legend-columns <number>
options.description: adjusts the number of legend columns (M, A; default: 2)

options.raw: legend-width <n> <units> = legend width (M, A; default: 14 cm)
options.name: legend-width <n> <units>
options.description: legend width (M, A; default: 14 cm)

options.raw: location <on/(off)> = set whether to export coordinates of cave entrances in the table.
options.name: location <on/(off)>
options.description: set whether to export coordinates of cave entrances in the table.

options.raw: map-comment <string> = optional comment displayed at the map header (M)
options.name: map-comment <string>
options.description: optional comment displayed at the map header (M)

options.raw: map-header <x> <y> <off/n/s/e/w/ne/nw/se/sw/center> = print map header at location specified by <x> <y>. Predefined map header contains some basic information about cave: name, scale, north arrow, list of surveyors etc. It is fully customizable (see the chapter Changing layout for details). <x> is easting (left-right on page). <y> is northing (up/down page). Ranges for <x> and <y> are -100--200. Lower-left corner of the map is 0 0, upper-right corner is 100 100. The header is aligned with the specified corner or side to this anchor point. (M; default: 0 100 nw)
options.name: map-header <x> <y> <off/n/s/e/w/ne/nw/se/sw/center>
options.description: print map header at location specified by <x> <y>. Predefined map header contains some basic information about cave: name, scale, north arrow, list of surveyors etc. It is fully customizable (see the chapter Changing layout for details). <x> is easting (left-right on page). <y> is northing (up/down page). Ranges for <x> and <y> are -100--200. Lower-left corner of the map is 0 0, upper-right corner is 100 100. The header is aligned with the specified corner or side to this anchor point. (M; default: 0 100 nw)

options.raw: map-header-bg <on/off> = when on, background of map header is filled with background color (e.g. to hide map grid). (M; default: off)
options.name: map-header-bg <on/off>
options.description: when on, background of map header is filled with background color (e.g. to hide map grid). (M; default: off)

options.raw: map-image <x> <y> <n/s/e/w/ne/nw/se/sw/center> <filename> = include image (Note that you can include PDF too, which may be used to combine plan and extended elevation into one nice looking PDF file.) specified by <filename> into map at location specified by <x> <y>. For coordinates and alignment details, see map-header specification.
options.name: map-image <x> <y> <n/s/e/w/ne/nw/se/sw/center> <filename>
options.description: include image (Note that you can include PDF too, which may be used to combine plan and extended elevation into one nice looking PDF file.) specified by <filename> into map at location specified by <x> <y>. For coordinates and alignment details, see map-header specification.

options.raw: map-level <number> = valid only when a map is selected. Determines the level at which map expansion for atlas export is stopped. By default 0 is used; if "basic" is specified, expansion is done up to the basic maps. Note: Map previews are displayed only as specified in maps in the current map-level.
options.name: map-level <number>
options.description: valid only when a map is selected. Determines the level at which map expansion for atlas export is stopped. By default 0 is used; if "basic" is specified, expansion is done up to the basic maps. Note: Map previews are displayed only as specified in maps in the current map-level.

options.raw: mark <keyword> = is used to mark the point on the line (see join command).
options.name: mark <keyword>
options.description: is used to mark the point on the line (see join command).

options.raw: min-symbol-scale <scale> = define minimal <scale>, from which points and lines are displayed on the map. E.g. for min-symbol-scale M, no points or lines scaled S and XS will be shown on the map. <scale> has the same format, as scale option for points and lines.
options.name: min-symbol-scale <scale>
options.description: define minimal <scale>, from which points and lines are displayed on the map. E.g. for min-symbol-scale M, no points or lines scaled S and XS will be shown on the map. <scale> has the same format, as scale option for points and lines.

options.raw: name <reference> = if the point type is station, this option gives the reference to the real survey station.
options.name: name <reference>
options.description: if the point type is station, this option gives the reference to the real survey station.

options.raw: namespace <on/off> = specify whether survey creates namespace (on by default)
options.name: namespace <on/off>
options.description: specify whether survey creates namespace (on by default)

options.raw: nav-factor <factor> = set atlas navigator zoom factor (A; default: 30)
options.name: nav-factor <factor>
options.description: set atlas navigator zoom factor (A; default: 30)

options.raw: nav-size <x-size> <y-size> = set number of atlas pages in both directions of navigator (A; default: 2 2)
options.name: nav-size <x-size> <y-size>
options.description: set number of atlas pages in both directions of navigator (A; default: 2 2)

options.raw: north <true/grid> = specify default orientation of the map. By default, true (astronomical) north is used. It is ignored when used with local coordinate system.
options.name: north <true/grid>
options.description: specify default orientation of the map. By default, true (astronomical) north is used. It is ignored when used with local coordinate system.

options.raw: opacity <value> = set opacity value (used if transparency is on). Value range is 0--100. (M, A; default: 70)
options.name: opacity <value>
options.description: set opacity value (used if transparency is on). Value range is 0--100. (M, A; default: 70)

options.raw: orientation/orient <number> = defines the orientation of the symbol. If not specified, it's oriented to north. 0 number < 360.
options.name: orientation/orient <number>
options.description: defines the orientation of the symbol. If not specified, it's oriented to north. 0 number < 360.

options.raw: origin <x> <y> <z> <units> = set origin of atlas pages (M, A)
options.name: origin <x> <y> <z> <units>
options.description: set origin of atlas pages (M, A)

options.raw: origin-label <x-label> <y-label> = set label for atlas page which has the lower left corner at the given origin coordinates. May be either a number or a string. (String labels form the following sequence, either in lower or upper case: A, B, ..., Z, AA, AB, ...) (M, A; default: 0 0)
options.name: origin-label <x-label> <y-label>
options.description: set label for atlas page which has the lower left corner at the given origin coordinates. May be either a number or a string. (String labels form the following sequence, either in lower or upper case: A, B, ..., Z, AA, AB, ...) (M, A; default: 0 0)

options.raw: outline <in/out/none> = determines whether the line serves as a border line for a scrap. Default value is "out" for walls, "none" for all other lines. Use -outline in for large pillars etc.
options.name: outline <in/out/none>
options.description: determines whether the line serves as a border line for a scrap. Default value is "out" for walls, "none" for all other lines. Use -outline in for large pillars etc.

options.raw: output/o <file> = set output file name. If no file name is given the prefix "cave." is used with an extension corresponding to output format. If the output filename is given and no output format is specified, the format is determined from the filename extension. model:
options.name: output/o <file>
options.description: set output file name. If no file name is given the prefix "cave." is used with an extension corresponding to output format. If the output filename is given and no output format is specified, the format is determined from the filename extension. model:

options.raw: overlap <value> <units> = set overlap size in paper units in the atlas mode or map margin in the map mode (M, A; default: 1 cm)
options.name: overlap <value> <units>
options.description: set overlap size in paper units in the atlas mode or map margin in the map mode (M, A; default: 1 cm)

options.raw: own-pages <number> = set number of own pages added before the first page of automatically generated pages in atlas mode (currently required for correct page numbering) (A; default: 0)
options.name: own-pages <number>
options.description: set number of own pages added before the first page of automatically generated pages in atlas mode (currently required for correct page numbering) (A; default: 0)

options.raw: -p <search-path> = This option is used to set the search path (or list of colon-separated paths) which therion uses to find its source files (if it doesn't find them in the working directory).
options.name: -p <search-path>
options.description: This option is used to set the search path (or list of colon-separated paths) which therion uses to find its source files (if it doesn't find them in the working directory).

options.raw: page-grid <on/off> = show pages key plan (M; default: off) map legend related:
options.name: page-grid <on/off>
options.description: show pages key plan (M; default: off) map legend related:

options.raw: page-numbers <on/off> = turn on/off page numbering (A; default: true)
options.name: page-numbers <on/off>
options.description: turn on/off page numbering (A; default: true)

options.raw: page-setup <dimensions> <units> = set page dimensions in this order: paper-width, paper-height, page-width, page-height, left-margin and top-margin. If not specified, it will be computed from size and overlap (A; default: 21 29.7 20 28.7 0.5 0.5 cm)
options.name: page-setup <dimensions> <units>
options.description: set page dimensions in this order: paper-width, paper-height, page-width, page-height, left-margin and top-margin. If not specified, it will be computed from size and overlap (A; default: 21 29.7 20 28.7 0.5 0.5 cm)

options.raw: person-rename <old name> <new name> = rename a person whose name has been changed
options.name: person-rename <old name> <new name>
options.description: rename a person whose name has been changed

options.raw: place <bottom/default/top> = changes displaying order in the map.
options.name: place <bottom/default/top>
options.description: changes displaying order in the map.

options.raw: preview <above/below> <other map id> = will put the outline of the other map in the specified preview position relative to the current map. Preview is displayed only if the map is in the map-level level as specified by the select command. Use the revise command if you want to add maps from higher levels to the preview.
options.name: preview <above/below> <other map id>
options.description: will put the outline of the other map in the specified preview position relative to the current map. Preview is displayed only if the map is in the map-level level as specified by the select command. Use the revise command if you want to add maps from higher levels to the preview.

options.raw: --print-encodings = Print a list of all supported input encodings.
options.name: --print-encodings
options.description: Print a list of all supported input encodings.

options.raw: --print-environment = Print environment settings for therion.
options.name: --print-environment
options.description: Print environment settings for therion.

options.raw: --print-init-file = Print a default initialization file. For more details see the Initialization section in the Appendix .
options.name: --print-init-file
options.description: Print a default initialization file. For more details see the Initialization section in the Appendix .

options.raw: --print-symbols = Print a list of all therion supported map symbols in symbols.xhtml file.
options.name: --print-symbols
options.description: Print a list of all therion supported map symbols in symbols.xhtml file.

options.raw: --print-tex-encodings = Print a list of all supported encodings for PDF output.
options.name: --print-tex-encodings
options.description: Print a list of all supported encodings for PDF output.

options.raw: projection <id> = required if the map contains survey.
options.name: projection <id>
options.description: required if the map contains survey.

options.raw: -q = Run therion in quiet mode. It will print only warning and error messages to STDERR.
options.name: -q
options.description: Run therion in quiet mode. It will print only warning and error messages to STDERR.

options.raw: rebelays <on/off> = this option can be specified only with the "rope" line type. Default is on.
options.name: rebelays <on/off>
options.description: this option can be specified only with the "rope" line type. Default is on.

options.raw: recursive <on/off> = valid only when a survey is selected. If set on (by default) all subsurveys of the given survey are recursively selected/unselected.
options.name: recursive <on/off>
options.description: valid only when a survey is selected. If set on (by default) all subsurveys of the given survey are recursively selected/unselected.

options.raw: --reproducible-output = Create reproducible PDF and SVG files. No volatile information is included (e.g. creation date or software version is omitted from the metadata). \ variables and are set to predefined constants. This option enforces the use of Therion loop closure. To get the same output files on different platforms, it's necessary to install the same set of basic fonts used by Therion (CM, CS and/or CMCYR font families; the ".pfb" font files have to be exactly the same on all platforms as they are embedded into PDF files.) and use reasonably recent \ distribution on all platforms.
options.name: --reproducible-output
options.description: Create reproducible PDF and SVG files. No volatile information is included (e.g. creation date or software version is omitted from the metadata). \ variables and are set to predefined constants. This option enforces the use of Therion loop closure. To get the same output files on different platforms, it's necessary to install the same set of basic fonts used by Therion (CM, CS and/or CMCYR font families; the ".pfb" font files have to be exactly the same on all platforms as they are embedded into PDF files.) and use reasonably recent \ distribution on all platforms.

options.raw: reverse <on/off> = whether points are given in reverse order.
options.name: reverse <on/off>
options.description: whether points are given in reverse order.

options.raw: rotate <value> = rotates the map (M, A; default: 0)
options.name: rotate <value>
options.description: rotates the map (M, A; default: 0)

options.raw: -s <source-file> = Set the name of the source file.
options.name: -s <source-file>
options.description: Set the name of the source file.

options.raw: scale = is used to pre-scale (convert coordinates from pixels to meters) the scrap data. If scrap projection is none, this is the only transformation that is done with coordinates. The <specification> has four forms: 1. <number> = <number> meters per drawing unit. 2. [<number> <length units>] = <number> <length units> per drawing unit. 3. [<num1> <num2> <length units>] = <num1> drawing units corresponds to <num2> <length units> in reality. 4. [<num1> ... <num8> [<length units>]] = this is the most general format, where you specify, in order, the x and y coordinates of two points in the scrap and two points in reality. Optionally, you can also specify units for the coordinates of the "points in reality". This form allows you to apply both scaling and rotation to the scrap.
options.name: scale
options.description: is used to pre-scale (convert coordinates from pixels to meters) the scrap data. If scrap projection is none, this is the only transformation that is done with coordinates. The <specification> has four forms: 1. <number> = <number> meters per drawing unit. 2. [<number> <length units>] = <number> <length units> per drawing unit. 3. [<num1> <num2> <length units>] = <num1> drawing units corresponds to <num2> <length units> in reality. 4. [<num1> ... <num8> [<length units>]] = this is the most general format, where you specify, in order, the x and y coordinates of two points in the scrap and two points in reality. Optionally, you can also specify units for the coordinates of the "points in reality". This form allows you to apply both scaling and rotation to the scrap.

options.raw: scale-bar <length> <units> = set the length of the scale-bar (M, A)
options.name: scale-bar <length> <units>
options.description: set the length of the scale-bar (M, A)

options.raw: scrap <reference> = if the point type is section, this is a reference to a cross-section scrap.
options.name: scrap <reference>
options.description: if the point type is section, this is a reference to a cross-section scrap.

options.raw: size <number> = set map size in the atlas mode. If not specified, it will be calculated from page-setup and overlap. In map mode applies iff page-grid is on (M, A; default: 18 22.2 cm)
options.name: size <number>
options.description: set map size in the atlas mode. If not specified, it will be calculated from page-setup and overlap. In map mode applies iff page-grid is on (M, A; default: 18 22.2 cm)

options.raw: sketch <filename> <x> <y> = underlying sketch bitmap specification (lower left corner coordinates).
options.name: sketch <filename> <x> <y>
options.description: underlying sketch bitmap specification (lower left corner coordinates).

options.raw: sketches <on/off> = turn on/off displaying of morphed sketch bitmaps. (M, A; default: off)
options.name: sketches <on/off>
options.description: turn on/off displaying of morphed sketch bitmaps. (M, A; default: off)

options.raw: smooth <on/off/auto> = indicates whether two lines are to be connected smoothly.
options.name: smooth <on/off/auto>
options.description: indicates whether two lines are to be connected smoothly.

options.raw: smooth-shading <off/quick> = set the mode of smooth scrap background shading. By default, altitude and depth colour is interpolated across the scrap the quick way. Some issues are present if transparent symbol colours are used. (Coloured scrap background is visible underneath the semitransparent areas, although only lower-scrap-drawings should be visible.) More precise modes should be added in the future. If off, scrap is filled with single colour.
options.name: smooth-shading <off/quick>
options.description: set the mode of smooth scrap background shading. By default, altitude and depth colour is interpolated across the scrap the quick way. Some issues are present if transparent symbol colours are used. (Coloured scrap background is visible underneath the semitransparent areas, although only lower-scrap-drawings should be visible.) More precise modes should be added in the future. If off, scrap is filled with single colour.

options.raw: station-names <prefix> <suffix> = adds given prefix/suffix to all survey stations in the current scrap. Saves some typing.
options.name: station-names <prefix> <suffix>
options.description: adds given prefix/suffix to all survey stations in the current scrap. Saves some typing.

options.raw: stations <list of station names> = stations you want to plot to the scrap, but which are not used for scrap transformation. You don't have to specify (draw) them with the point station command.
options.name: stations <list of station names>
options.description: stations you want to plot to the scrap, but which are not used for scrap transformation. You don't have to specify (draw) them with the point station command.

options.raw: statistics <carto/copyright-count on/hide/off> = display number of scraps for given author/copyright string; if set to off, items are sorted alphabetically; otherwise by number of scraps (M, A; default: hide)
options.name: statistics <carto/copyright-count on/hide/off>
options.description: display number of scraps for given author/copyright string; if set to off, items are sorted alphabetically; otherwise by number of scraps (M, A; default: hide)

options.raw: subtype <restr_keyword> = determines line subtype. The following subtypes are supported for given types: wall: invisible, bedrock (default), sand, clay, pebbles, debris, blocks, ice, underlying, overlying, unsurveyed, presumed, pit (Usually open to surface.) , flowstone, moonmilk; border: visible (default), invisible, temporary, presumed; water-flow: permanent (default), conjectural, intermittent; survey: cave (default), surface (default if centreline has surface flag). The subtype may be specified also directly in <type> specification using ":" as a separator. (E.g. border:invisible) Any subtype specification can be used with user defined type (u). In this case you need also to define corresponding metapost symbol (see the chapter New map symbols ).
options.name: subtype <restr_keyword>
options.description: determines line subtype. The following subtypes are supported for given types: wall: invisible, bedrock (default), sand, clay, pebbles, debris, blocks, ice, underlying, overlying, unsurveyed, presumed, pit (Usually open to surface.) , flowstone, moonmilk; border: visible (default), invisible, temporary, presumed; water-flow: permanent (default), conjectural, intermittent; survey: cave (default), surface (default if centreline has surface flag). The subtype may be specified also directly in <type> specification using ":" as a separator. (E.g. border:invisible) Any subtype specification can be used with user defined type (u). In this case you need also to define corresponding metapost symbol (see the chapter New map symbols ).

options.raw: surface <top/bottom/off> = set the position of the surface bitmap above/below the map. (M, A; default: off)
options.name: surface <top/bottom/off>
options.description: set the position of the surface bitmap above/below the map. (M, A; default: off)

options.raw: surface-opacity <value> = set the surface bitmap opacity (used if transparency is on). Value range is 0--100. (M, A; default: 70)
options.name: surface-opacity <value>
options.description: set the surface bitmap opacity (used if transparency is on). Value range is 0--100. (M, A; default: 70)

options.raw: survey <id> = associate a survey with map (e.g.\ all surveying statistics from this survey will be used when this map is selected for output).
options.name: survey <id>
options.description: associate a survey with map (e.g.\ all surveying statistics from this survey will be used when this map is selected for output).

options.raw: survey-level <N/all> = N is the number of survey levels displayed next to the station name (M, A; default: 0). PDF related:
options.name: survey-level <N/all>
options.description: N is the number of survey levels displayed next to the station name (M, A; default: 0). PDF related:

options.raw: surveys (create)/use/ignore = exports raw list of caves when set off. Otherwise survey structure with aggregated statistics is also displayed. database:
options.name: surveys (create)/use/ignore
options.description: exports raw list of caves when set off. Otherwise survey structure with aggregated statistics is also displayed. database:

options.raw: symbol-colo[u]r <point/line/area/group/special> <symbol-type> <colour> = change colour of particular symbol or group of symbols. (Note: colour change currently applies to pattern fills only if (1) output format is PDF and (2) \ version is at least 1.000) (M, A)
options.name: symbol-colo[u]r <point/line/area/group/special> <symbol-type> <colour>
options.description: change colour of particular symbol or group of symbols. (Note: colour change currently applies to pattern fills only if (1) output format is PDF and (2) \ version is at least 1.000) (M, A)

options.raw: symbol-hide <point/line/area/group/special> <symbol-type> = don't display particular symbol or group of symbols. You may use group cave-centerline, group surface-centerline, point cave-station, point surface-station and group text in symbol-hide and symbol-show commands. Use flag:<entrance/continuation/sink/spring/doline/dig> as a <symbol-type> to hide stations with particular flags (e.g. symbol-hide point flag:entrance). May be combined with symbol-show.(M, A)
options.name: symbol-hide <point/line/area/group/special> <symbol-type>
options.description: don't display particular symbol or group of symbols. You may use group cave-centerline, group surface-centerline, point cave-station, point surface-station and group text in symbol-hide and symbol-show commands. Use flag:<entrance/continuation/sink/spring/doline/dig> as a <symbol-type> to hide stations with particular flags (e.g. symbol-hide point flag:entrance). May be combined with symbol-show.(M, A)

options.raw: symbol-set <symbol-set> = use symbol-set for all map symbols, if available. Be aware, that symbol set name is case sensitive. (M, A) Therion uses following predefined symbol sets: UIS (International Union of Speleology) ASF (Australian Speleological Federation) AUT (Austrian Speleological Association) BCRA (British Cave Research Association ) NSS (National Speleological Society/USA) NZSS (New Zealand Symbol Set) SBE (Brazilian Speleological Society - Sociedade Brasileira de Espeleologia ) SKBB (Speleoklub Bansk\'a Bystrica)
options.name: symbol-set <symbol-set>
options.description: use symbol-set for all map symbols, if available. Be aware, that symbol set name is case sensitive. (M, A) Therion uses following predefined symbol sets: UIS (International Union of Speleology) ASF (Australian Speleological Federation) AUT (Austrian Speleological Association) BCRA (British Cave Research Association ) NSS (National Speleological Society/USA) NZSS (New Zealand Symbol Set) SBE (Brazilian Speleological Society - Sociedade Brasileira de Espeleologia ) SKBB (Speleoklub Bansk\'a Bystrica)

options.raw: symbol-show <point/line/area/group/special> <symbol-type> = display particular symbol or group of symbols. May be combined with symbol-hide. (M, A)
options.name: symbol-show <point/line/area/group/special> <symbol-type>
options.description: display particular symbol or group of symbols. May be combined with symbol-hide. (M, A)

options.raw:
text = text of the label, remark or continuation. It may contain following formatting keywords: (For SVG output, only
, , , , and keywords are taken into account; all others are silently ignored.)
= line break
/, , = line alignment for multi-line labels. Ignored if there is no
tag. = thin space , , , , = font switches and = marks beginning and end of a right-to-left written text = creates multilingual label (see string type for detailed description) = specify the font size in points; N should be an integer between 1 and 127.
options.name: text
options.description:
text of the label, remark or continuation. It may contain following formatting keywords: (For SVG output, only
, , , , and keywords are taken into account; all others are silently ignored.)
= line break
/, , = line alignment for multi-line labels. Ignored if there is no
tag. = thin space , , , , = font switches and = marks beginning and end of a right-to-left written text = creates multilingual label (see string type for detailed description) = specify the font size in points; N should be an integer between 1 and 127.

options.raw: title <string> = description of the object
options.name: title <string>
options.description: description of the object

options.raw: title-pages <on/off> = turn on/off title pages before each atlas chapter (A; default: off)
options.name: title-pages <on/off>
options.description: turn on/off title pages before each atlas chapter (A; default: off)

options.raw: transparency <on/off> = set transparency for the passages (underlying passages are also visible) (M, A; default: on)
options.name: transparency <on/off>
options.description: set transparency for the passages (underlying passages are also visible) (M, A; default: on)

options.raw: units <metric/imperial> = set output units (M, A; default: metric)
options.name: units <metric/imperial>
options.description: set output units (M, A; default: metric)

options.raw: --use-extern-libs = Don't copy \ and \ macros to working directory. \ and \ should search for them on their own. Use with caution.
options.name: --use-extern-libs
options.description: Don't copy \ and \ macros to working directory. \ and \ should search for them on their own. Use with caution.

options.raw: -v, --version = Display version information.
options.name: -v, --version
options.description: Display version information.

options.raw: value = value of height, passage-height, altitude, dimensions or date height: according to the sign of the value (positive, negative or unsigned), this type of symbol represents chimney height, pit depth or step height in general. The numeric value can be optionally followed by "?", if the value is presumed and units can be added (e.g. -value [40? ft]). passage-height: the following four forms of value are supported: +<number> (the height of the ceiling), -<number> (the depth of the floor or water depth), <number> (the distance between floor and ceiling) and [+<number> -<number>] (the distance to ceiling and distance to floor). All four forms can be optionally followed by a unit. If there is more than one piece of information - two numbers, number and unit or else - the value must be enclosed in square brackets. altitude: the value specified is the altitude difference from the nearest station. The value will be set to 0 if defined as "-", ".", "nan", "NAN" or "NaN". If the altitude value is prefixed by "fix" (e.g. -value [fix 1300]), this value is used as an absolute altitude. The value can optionally be followed by length units. dimensions: -value [<above> <below> [<units>]] specifies passage dimensions a\-bo\-ve/below centerline plane used in 3D model. date: -value <date> sets the date for the date point.
options.name: value
options.description: value of height, passage-height, altitude, dimensions or date height: according to the sign of the value (positive, negative or unsigned), this type of symbol represents chimney height, pit depth or step height in general. The numeric value can be optionally followed by "?", if the value is presumed and units can be added (e.g. -value [40? ft]). passage-height: the following four forms of value are supported: +<number> (the height of the ceiling), -<number> (the depth of the floor or water depth), <number> (the distance between floor and ceiling) and [+<number> -<number>] (the distance to ceiling and distance to floor). All four forms can be optionally followed by a unit. If there is more than one piece of information - two numbers, number and unit or else - the value must be enclosed in square brackets. altitude: the value specified is the altitude difference from the nearest station. The value will be set to 0 if defined as "-", ".", "nan", "NAN" or "NaN". If the altitude value is prefixed by "fix" (e.g. -value [fix 1300]), this value is used as an absolute altitude. The value can optionally be followed by length units. dimensions: -value [<above> <below> [<units>]] specifies passage dimensions a\-bo\-ve/below centerline plane used in 3D model. date: -value <date> sets the date for the date point.

options.raw: --verify-output-crc = Verify that the output file has not been changed. Generate reproducible output, calculate CRC32 checksum and check it against one saved in ".crc" file.
options.name: --verify-output-crc
options.description: Verify that the output file has not been changed. Generate reproducible output, calculate CRC32 checksum and check it against one saved in ".crc" file.

options.raw: visibility <on/off> = displays/hides the symbol.
options.name: visibility <on/off>
options.description: displays/hides the symbol.

options.raw: wall-source <maps/centerline/all/splays> = set source data for passage wall modeling. map/atlas:
options.name: wall-source <maps/centerline/all/splays>
options.description: set source data for passage wall modeling. map/atlas:

options.raw: walls <on/off/auto> = specify if the scrap should be used in 3D model reconstruction
options.name: walls <on/off/auto>
options.description: specify if the scrap should be used in 3D model reconstruction

options.raw: -x = Generate file ".xtherion.dat" with additional information for XTherion.
